name: Mom's Cinnamon Rolls (Dorothy Schmidt)
recipeInstructions:
1. These rolls have a lot of layers due to how thin the dough is rolled out.
2. Mrs. Schmidt says that beating the ingredients thoroughly makes this recipe work well.
3. There's also a trick for getting the sprinkled water just right—just a few drops here and there.
4. Sprinkle yeast in 3 cups warm water with 1 tablespoon sugar, mix, and let sit for 10 minutes.
5. Add 3 cups flour and beat well with a beater.
6. Let rise until spongy (30 minutes).
7. Beat 3 eggs thoroughly for about 5 minutes (foamy and fluffy).
8. Soften the margarine and add 1 cup sugar, beating very well until very creamy.
9. Add to the eggs and beat until very well blended and then add salt.
10. Add egg mixture to sponge mixture.
11. Gradually add approximately four to five cups flour, mixing one cup at a time with a wooden spoon.
12. When it is hard to stir, transfer the dough to a board and knead until not sticky and easy to handle (you don't have to knead all the flour).
13. Place the dough in a warm oven and cover with a damp cloth; let rise for about 1 hour until doubled.
14. Prepare pans by greasing 2 large rectangular baking pans with margarine, sprinkling liberally (not packed) with brown sugar (about 1/4 inch deep), and dotting with butter (1 stick per pan).
15. Sprinkle very lightly with water to keep the sugar from burning and sticking.
16. Punch down dough and let rise for another half hour.
17. Turn out the dough and take about half of it, rolling it out to a 1/2-inch thick rectangle.
18. Spread with soft butter (covering all of the rectangle) and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.
19. Roll up in a jelly roll style, stretching the dough while rolling.
20. Cut into 1-inch slices.
21. Place in pans, touching only slightly, and let rise (covered with a damp cloth) until above the pans.
22. Bake at 325-350°F for 20-25 minutes.
